--- original
+++ modified
@@ -8,15 +8,15 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: a780
 project: spatial-nav
-title: 'Clean up: rename claimWhen to navOverride, remove bulk predicate code'
+title: 'Clean up: rename claimWhen to navOverride, layer-scoped, remove bulk predicate code'
 ---
 ## What

-After all views are migrated to spatial navigation, clean up the predicate infrastructure and replace the broadcast-based `claimWhen` with a simpler, targeted override model.
+After all views are migrated to spatial navigation, clean up the predicate infrastructure and replace the broadcast-based `claimWhen` with a simpler, targeted override model — **scoped to the focused entry's layer**.

 ### New override model

-The old `claimWhen` was pull-based: every scope registers predicates, `broadcastNavCommand` walks all of them on every keystroke — O(all predicates). The new model is **targeted**: overrides live on the focused scope only, checked as a lookup table before spatial nav.
+The old `claimWhen` was pull-based: every scope registers predicates, `broadcastNavCommand` walks all of them on every keystroke — O(all predicates). The new model is **targeted**: overrides live on the focused scope only, checked as a lookup table before spatial nav runs.

 **Interface change:**

@@ -28,14 +28,61 @@
 navOverride={{ "nav.right": "task:02", "nav.left": null }}
 ```

-- **String value**: "go here instead of spatial nav"
+- **String value (moniker)**: "go here instead of spatial nav"
 - **`null` value**: "block this direction, don't navigate"
 - **Missing key**: "use spatial nav (default)"

-**Rust-side**: The override map is stored in `SpatialEntry.overrides: HashMap<Direction, Option<String>>`. The `navigate()` function checks the focused entry's overrides first. If an override exists for the requested direction, return it (or None for null/blocked). Only if no override exists does the beam test + scoring run.
+### Layer scoping of override targets
+
+An override target moniker is **resolved within the focused entry's layer**. If the target exists but lives in a different layer, the override is **ignored** (no-op) and spatial nav runs as usual — cross-layer teleportation is never allowed, even via override.
+
+Rationale: the whole point of the layer model is that layer boundaries are absolute. An override that points across a layer would punch through that guarantee and create surprising behavior (e.g. an inspector field's override moniker still resolves after the inspector closes, jumping to the board).
+
+### Rust side
+
+The override map is stored in `SpatialEntry.overrides: HashMap<Direction, Option<String>>`:
+
+```rust
+pub struct SpatialEntry {
+    pub moniker: String,
+    pub rect: Rect,
+    pub layer_key: LayerKey,
+    pub parent_scope: Option<String>,
+    pub overrides: HashMap<Direction, Option<String>>,  // Option<String> = moniker or null=blocked
+}
+```
+
+The `navigate()` function (card `01KNQXXF5W7G4JP73C6ZCMKYKX`) gets an override check before beam test:

-**React-side**: `navOverride` prop on FocusScope is a `Record<string, string | null>`. FocusScope sends it to Rust via `spatial_register`. No more predicate registry, no more `registerClaimPredicates` / `unregisterClaimPredicates` / `claimPredicatesRef`.
+```rust
+pub fn navigate(&self, key: SpatialKey, direction: Direction) -> Option<String> {
+    let focused = self.entries.get(&key)?;

+    // Check override first
+    if let Some(override_moniker) = focused.overrides.get(&direction) {
+        match override_moniker {
+            None => return None,  // explicit block
+            Some(moniker) => {
+                // Resolve only within focused's layer — cross-layer overrides are ignored
+                if self.entries_in_layer(&focused.layer_key)
+                    .any(|e| &e.moniker == moniker)
+                {
+                    return Some(moniker.clone());
+                }
+                // Target not in this layer — fall through to beam/score
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    // Default: beam test + scoring, layer-scoped (unchanged)
+    self.beam_score(focused, direction)
+}
+```
+
+### React side
+
+`navOverride` prop on FocusScope is a `Record<string, string | null>`. FocusScope sends it to Rust via `spatial_register`. No more predicate registry, no more `registerClaimPredicates` / `unregisterClaimPredicates` / `claimPredicatesRef`.
+
 This matches how every other system works: Android's `nextFocusRight` is a property on the view, UWP's `XYFocusRight` is a property on the element. Not a global listener.

 ### Clean up
@@ -45,20 +92,21 @@
    - Remove `ClaimPredicate` interface
    - Remove `claimPredicatesRef`, `registerClaimPredicates`, `unregisterClaimPredicates`
    - Remove the predicate walk from `broadcastNavCommand` (spatial nav is the only path now, with overrides checked in Rust)
-3. **Remove `useRestoreFocus`** — FocusLayer's focus memory replaces this
+3. **Remove `useRestoreFocus`** — FocusLayer's `last_focused` replaces this (done in card `01KNQXYC4RBQP1N2NQ33P8DPB9`; confirm removal here)
 4. **Send overrides to Rust** via `spatial_register` — the override map travels with the spatial entry

 ### Subtasks
 - [ ] Replace `claimWhen: ClaimPredicate[]` with `navOverride: Record<string, string | null>` on FocusScope
 - [ ] Add `overrides: HashMap<Direction, Option<String>>` to `SpatialEntry` in Rust
-- [ ] Update `navigate()` to check focused entry's overrides before beam test
+- [ ] Update `navigate()` to check focused entry's overrides before beam test, scoped to its layer
 - [ ] Delete `ClaimPredicate` type, predicate registry, and broadcast walk from entity-focus-context
-- [ ] Remove `useRestoreFocus` hook (replaced by FocusLayer focus memory)
+- [ ] Confirm `useRestoreFocus` deleted

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `claimWhen` prop removed, replaced by `navOverride` with simple directive map
 - [ ] Override check is O(1) — lookup on focused entry, no broadcast walk
-- [ ] `navOverride={{ "nav.right": "task:02" }}` sends focus to task:02 on nav.right
+- [ ] `navOverride={{ "nav.right": "task:02" }}` sends focus to task:02 **if task:02 exists in the same layer**
+- [ ] `navOverride={{ "nav.right": "task:02" }}` where task:02 exists only in another layer → override ignored, spatial nav runs
 - [ ] `navOverride={{ "nav.left": null }}` blocks nav.left (no movement)
 - [ ] Missing direction key → spatial nav (default behavior)
 - [ ] Overrides stored in Rust `SpatialEntry`, checked in `navigate()` before beam test
@@ -67,9 +115,10 @@
 - [ ] `cargo test` passes, `pnpm vitest run` passes

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Rust unit tests` — navigate with override returns override target
-- [ ] `Rust unit tests` — navigate with null override returns None (blocked)
-- [ ] `Rust unit tests` — navigate with no override for direction falls through to spatial
+- [ ] Rust: navigate with same-layer override returns override target moniker
+- [ ] Rust: navigate with null override returns None (blocked)
+- [ ] Rust: navigate with override pointing to cross-layer moniker → override ignored, beam test runs
+- [ ] Rust: navigate with no override for direction falls through to spatial
 - [ ] `focus-scope.test.tsx` — navOverride prop accepted and sent to Rust
 - [ ] All navigation works via spatial nav with no overrides in production code
 - [ ] Run `cargo test` and `cd kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run` — all pass
